summary: Set 045 Session 1 of 6 — open-question spike + joiner location decision. Scope locked at start-of-set as spike-only (no production-grade dabbler-launch CLI in S1; that ships in S3). Four open empirical questions from Set 044 proposal v1 §6 resolved sufficiently for S2-S6 to proceed: (Q1 bypass-rate) clock-started; designed schema + capture protocol for ~/.dabbler/bypass-observation-log.jsonl (operator-local, NOT committed); wrote the 2026-05-24 clock-start entry; 14-day observation window through 2026-06-07 with end-of-day reflective capture; analysis at S5 start. (Q2 deterministic correlation) wrote spike-prototypes/correlation_prototype.py and demonstrated 1:1 binding against real on-disk Claude (~/.claude/projects/) and Copilot (~/.copilot/session-state/) logs via (workspace_cwd canonical, time_window, conv_id) keys; positive cases bound to ef28a7ff-... (Claude) + 5ef62da5-... (Copilot); 2s window misses (Claude first-event lags subprocess spawn ~5s); 30s window safe; 1h ambiguity probe still 1:1 in busy workspaces. (Q3 Claude phrasing-trigger) authored claude_phrasing_ablation_analysis.md — diffed v1 (refused) vs v2 (accepted on session-start) CLAUDE.md texts; 7 distinguishing elements ranked; strongest hypotheses H1 (harvest lexical family alone) + H8 (harvest + pretense composite); 4 defensive canonical-template rules sufficient for S4 to ship CLAUDE.md without running the optional A2-A8 ablation; per-turn skip already absorbed by proposal v1.1 (session-start-only). (Q4 joiner location) built parallel Python + TypeScript joiner sketches on engine-mismatch conflict scenario; both correctly detect synthetic conflict + clear control; Python scans 461 native sessions in 36 ms vs TypeScript 2,589 ms (~70x slower, idiomatic comparison); LOCKED to Python at ai_router.joiner; rubric: reuse of ai_router state-file + lifecycle infra, pytest testability, cross-tier Lightweight reuse, perf, debuggability outweigh TS's IPC + file-watching wins. Pass A (Set 044) consensus 2-1 Python confirmed. spec.md S1 block rewritten to lock the spike-only descope. Step 5 pytest 693 passed + 1 skipped (no regressions). Step 6 verification: GPT-5.4 sustained 429s on OpenAI endpoint (2 attempts, smaller bundle 2nd time); pivoted to Gemini Pro via in-process router-config monkey-patch (still cross-provider from Anthropic); VERIFIED with no issues — verifier explicitly endorsed Q2 window-sizing + cwd-canonicalization + ambiguity handling, Q4 rubric + benchmark + IPC argument, Q3 hypothesis matrix + defensive rules sufficiency, Q1 protocol + schema. Cumulative routed Session 1: $0.024 of $5 NTE budget. Carry-forward: S2 (joiner design + canonical schema) starts from locked Python location + engine-mismatch sketch + Q2 correlation join keys; S3 (wrapper + Copilot parser) consumes the canonical schema once S2 derives it from joiner needs; S4 (Claude parser + narration v1.1 template) consumes the 4 defensive Claude template rules; S5 (Explorer integration) reads the bypass-observation-log for the fraction analysis. ai-assignment.md self-authored disclosure preserved (operator standing in-session router restriction; lessons-learned bias caveat acknowledged).

reason.specifics: Session 2 (Joiner design + canonical schema) is the engineering center of gravity for Set 045 per the Set 044 Pass B consensus (the joiner's conflict-detection semantics drive the canonical Harvest Record schema, NOT the other way around). Same Claude orchestrator preserves continuity with the locked Q4 Python decision + the Q2 correlation prototype's edge-case findings + the engine-mismatch conflict sketch from this session. The work is dense schema + semantics authoring against the spec.md S2 block; Opus's careful-reasoning profile fits better than a Codex/Gemini handoff that would need to re-load Set 044 + Set 045 S1 context. This recommendation is SELF-AUTHORED per the operator standing in-session router restriction (same disclosure as ai-assignment.md S1 block); when the restriction is lifted, S2 start-of-session should re-route the recommendation for an independent read. The Set 044 Pass A 2-1 Python lock was already a routed cross-provider decision, so the high-leverage architectural call this set inherits is already vetted; S2's next call is on the joiner's conflict-detection enumeration which is internal to Python + spec authoring.
